<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:スセリビメ]][[sv:Suseri-Hime]][[zh:須世理姬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:スセリビメ]][[sv:Suseri-Hime]][[zh:須世理姬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:スセリビメ]][[sv:Suseri-Hime]][[zh:須世理姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|father={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}|relatives={{ill|Oshihomimi|WD=Q462555}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q8191715]] Wikidata name: Suseri-hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Susanoo | successor = | predecessor= | pantheon = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Susanoo-no-Mikoto | aliases = Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Suserihime, Wakasuserihime | titles = | god_of = | tomb = | parents = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (father) | consort = [[:en:Ōnamuchi|Ōkuninushi]] | children = None | worshiped = [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], among others | name = Suseribime-no-Mikoto | predecessor = | successor = | classification = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 須勢理毘売命 (Suseribime-no-Mikoto) | aliases = 須勢理毘売, 須世理毘売, 須世理姫, 和加須世理比売命 | other names = | divine Rank = | domains = | shrine = {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha}} and others | father = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} | mother = | spouse = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} | children = None }}
'''Suseribime''' (須勢理毘売, 須世理毘売, 須世理姫, 須世理比売, 和加須世理比売命) is a female deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, she is named as '''須勢理毘売命''' (Suseribime-no-Mikoto) or '''須世理毘売''' (Suseribime). In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, she is referred to as '''須世理姫''' (Suserihime), and in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, she is described as '''和加須世理比売命''' (Waka Suserihime-no-Mikoto).
Although her origins are unclear, Suseribime is the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the principal wife of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.
== Mythology == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Suseribime resides in the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|en|Ne-no-kuni|WD=Q7555425|lt=Ne-no-Kuni}} (the underworld) with her father, Susanoo. She encounters Ōnamuchi (Ōkuninushi) when he flees from the pursuit of the {{ill|Yaso-no-Kami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}. The two marry immediately. Upon returning home, Suseribime introduces Ōnamuchi to her father. Susanoo tests him by placing him in perilous situations, such as sleeping in a room filled with snakes, bees, and centipedes. Suseribime assists Ōnamuchi by providing him with enchanted items, such as the '''Hire''' (比礼), to help him survive.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi ties Susanoo’s hair to a pillar and escapes with Suseribime. As he departs, Susanoo names Ōnamuchi as "Ōkuninushi" and blesses their union, instructing him to build a grand palace and pacify the land.
Although Ōkuninushi had children with {{ill|Yakami-hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakami-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}} before meeting Suseribime, Yakami-hime feared Suseribime’s authority and left, entrusting their child, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata-no-Kami|qq=}}, to Ōkuninushi.
Later, Suseribime exhibits intense jealousy when Ōkuninushi courts {{ill|Nunakawahime|en|Nunakawahime|WD=Q11142832|lt=Numakawa-hime}} in {{ill|Koshi Province (Japan)|en|Koshi Province (Japan)|WD=Q1351611|lt=Koshi Province}}. Despite her jealousy, the two reconcile and remain together.
== Analysis == The name "Suseri" may derive from "susu" (advance) and "susa" (rage), indicating her passionate nature. Her actions, whether helping her husband overcome trials or expressing jealousy, demonstrate her immense authority and influence as a deity.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki</ref>
The story of Suseribime and Ōkuninushi fits the framework of a "marriage test" myth, where the groom undergoes trials set by the bride’s father. Successfully completing these trials signifies the groom’s worthiness.<ref name="bunken1">*Nihon Shinwa Jiten*, p. 182</ref>
== Shrines == Suseribime is often worshipped alongside Ōkuninushi. Notable shrines include: * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Ōkami Ōgū Shrine * {{ill|Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namesa Shrine|qq=}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} (Nara City, Nara Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Meoto Daikokusha * {{ill|Kunikami Shrine|ja|國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikami Shrine|qq=}} (Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社}} (Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Bitchū-no-kuni Sōjagū|ja|備中国総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bitchū Sōja Shrine|qq=}} (Sōja City, Okayama Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐乃男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}

Susanoo tests him by placing him in perilous situations, such as sleeping in a room filled with snakes, bees, and centipedes. Suseribime assists Ōnamuchi by providing him with enchanted items, such as the '''Hire''' (比礼), to help him survive.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi ties Susanoo’s hair to a pillar and escapes with Suseribime. As he departs, Susanoo names Ōnamuchi as "Ōkuninushi" and blesses their union, instructing him to build a grand palace and pacify the land.
Although Ōkuninushi had children with {{ill|Yakami-hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakami-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}} before meeting Suseribime, Yakami-hime feared Suseribime’s authority and left, entrusting their child, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata-no-Kami|qq=}}, to Ōkuninushi.
Later, Suseribime exhibits intense jealousy when Ōkuninushi courts {{ill|Nunakawahime|en|Nunakawahime|WD=Q11142832|lt=Numakawa-hime}} in {{ill|Koshi Province (Japan)|en|Koshi Province (Japan)|WD=Q1351611|lt=Koshi Province}}. Despite her jealousy, the two reconcile and remain together.
== Analysis == The name "Suseri" may derive from "susu" (advance) and "susa" (rage), indicating her passionate nature. Her actions, whether helping her husband overcome trials or expressing jealousy, demonstrate her immense authority and influence as a deity.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki</ref>
The story of Suseribime and Ōkuninushi fits the framework of a "marriage test" myth, where the groom undergoes trials set by the bride’s father. Successfully completing these trials signifies the groom’s worthiness.<ref name="bunken1">*Nihon Shinwa Jiten*, p. 182</ref>
== Shrines == Suseribime is often worshipped alongside Ōkuninushi. Notable shrines include: * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Ōkami Ōgū Shrine * {{ill|Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namesa Shrine|qq=}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} (Nara City, Nara Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Meoto Daikokusha * {{ill|Kunikami Shrine|ja|國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikami Shrine|qq=}} (Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社}} (Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Bitchū-no-kuni Sōjagū|ja|備中国総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bitchū Sōja Shrine|qq=}} (Sōja City, Okayama Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐乃男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}

'''Suseribime''' (須勢理毘売, 須世理毘売, 須世理姫, 須世理比売, 和加須世理比売命) is a female deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, she is named as '''須勢理毘売命''' (Suseribime-no-Mikoto) or '''須世理毘売''' (Suseribime). In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, she is referred to as '''須世理姫''' (Suserihime), and in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, she is described as '''和加須世理比売命''' (Waka Suserihime-no-Mikoto).
Although her origins are unclear, Suseribime is the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the principal wife of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.
== Mythology == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Suseribime resides in the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|en|Ne-no-kuni|WD=Q7555425|lt=Ne-no-Kuni}} (the underworld) with her father, Susanoo. She encounters Ōnamuchi (Ōkuninushi) when he flees from the pursuit of the {{ill|Yaso-no-Kami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}. The two marry immediately. Upon returning home, Suseribime introduces Ōnamuchi to her father. Susanoo tests him by placing him in perilous situations, such as sleeping in a room filled with snakes, bees, and centipedes. Suseribime assists Ōnamuchi by providing him with enchanted items, such as the '''Hire''' (比礼), to help him survive.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi ties Susanoo’s hair to a pillar and escapes with Suseribime. As he departs, Susanoo names Ōnamuchi as "Ōkuninushi" and blesses their union, instructing him to build a grand palace and pacify the land.
Although Ōkuninushi had children with {{ill|Yakami-hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakami-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}} before meeting Suseribime, Yakami-hime feared Suseribime’s authority and left, entrusting their child, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata-no-Kami|qq=}}, to Ōkuninushi.
Later, Suseribime exhibits intense jealousy when Ōkuninushi courts {{ill|Nunakawahime|en|Nunakawahime|WD=Q11142832|lt=Numakawa-hime}} in {{ill|Koshi Province (Japan)|en|Koshi Province (Japan)|WD=Q1351611|lt=Koshi Province}}. Despite her jealousy, the two reconcile and remain together.
== Analysis == The name "Suseri" may derive from "susu" (advance) and "susa" (rage), indicating her passionate nature. Her actions, whether helping her husband overcome trials or expressing jealousy, demonstrate her immense authority and influence as a deity.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki</ref>
The story of Suseribime and Ōkuninushi fits the framework of a "marriage test" myth, where the groom undergoes trials set by the bride’s father. Successfully completing these trials signifies the groom’s worthiness.<ref name="bunken1">*Nihon Shinwa Jiten*, p. 182</ref>
== Shrines == Suseribime is often worshipped alongside Ōkuninushi. Notable shrines include: * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Ōkami Ōgū Shrine * {{ill|Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namesa Shrine|qq=}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} (Nara City, Nara Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Meoto Daikokusha * {{ill|Kunikami Shrine|ja|國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikami Shrine|qq=}} (Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社}} (Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Bitchū-no-kuni Sōjagū|ja|備中国総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bitchū Sōja Shrine|qq=}} (Sōja City, Okayama Prefecture)

'''Suseribime''' (須勢理毘売, 須世理毘売, 須世理姫, 須世理比売, 和加須世理比売命) is a female deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, she is named as '''須勢理毘売命''' (Suseribime-no-Mikoto) or '''須世理毘売''' (Suseribime). In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, she is referred to as '''須世理姫''' (Suserihime), and in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, she is described as '''和加須世理比売命''' (Waka Suserihime-no-Mikoto).
Although her origins are unclear, Suseribime is the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the principal wife of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.
== Mythology == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Suseribime resides in the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|en|Ne-no-kuni|WD=Q7555425|lt=Ne-no-Kuni|1=Ne-no-kuni}} (the underworld) with her father, Susanoo. She encounters Ōnamuchi (Ōkuninushi) when he flees from the pursuit of the {{ill|Yaso-no-Kami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}. The two marry immediately. Upon returning home, Suseribime introduces Ōnamuchi to her father. Susanoo tests him by placing him in perilous situations, such as sleeping in a room filled with snakes, bees, and centipedes. Suseribime assists Ōnamuchi by providing him with enchanted items, such as the '''Hire''' (比礼), to help him survive.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi ties Susanoo’s hair to a pillar and escapes with Suseribime. As he departs, Susanoo names Ōnamuchi as "Ōkuninushi" and blesses their union, instructing him to build a grand palace and pacify the land.
Although Ōkuninushi had children with {{ill|Yakami-hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakami-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}} before meeting Suseribime, Yakami-hime feared Suseribime’s authority and left, entrusting their child, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata-no-Kami|qq=}}, to Ōkuninushi.
Later, Suseribime exhibits intense jealousy when Ōkuninushi courts {{ill|Nunakawahime|en|Nunakawahime|WD=Q11142832|lt=Numakawa-hime|1=Nunakawahime}} in {{ill|Koshi Province (Japan)|en|Koshi Province (Japan)|WD=Q1351611|lt=Koshi Province|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}}. Despite her jealousy, the two reconcile and remain together.
== Analysis == The name "Suseri" may derive from "susu" (advance) and "susa" (rage), indicating her passionate nature. Her actions, whether helping her husband overcome trials or expressing jealousy, demonstrate her immense authority and influence as a deity.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki</ref>
The story of Suseribime and Ōkuninushi fits the framework of a "marriage test" myth, where the groom undergoes trials set by the bride’s father. Successfully completing these trials signifies the groom’s worthiness.<ref name="bunken1">*Nihon Shinwa Jiten*, p. 182</ref>
== Shrines == Suseribime is often worshipped alongside Ōkuninushi. Notable shrines include: * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha|1=Izumo-taisha}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Ōkami Ōgū Shrine * {{ill|Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namesa Shrine|qq=}} (Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}} (Nara City, Nara Prefecture) – Sub-shrine: Meoto Daikokusha * {{ill|Kunikami Shrine|ja|國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikami Shrine|qq=}} (Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社}} (Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Bitchū-no-kuni Sōjagū|ja|備中国総社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bitchū Sōja Shrine|qq=}} (Sōja City, Okayama Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐乃男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}
